notes: PDF output is deferred to a later update; assertions target markdown + CSV only.

evals.prompt: Make a rubric for a 9th grade persuasive essay on climate policy, aligned to DOK, 4 criteria, 4 levels, 100 points.

assertions.note: exactly 4 criteria rows and 4 level columns (plus 1 criterion column)
every criterion x level cell is non-empty
every criterion row carries a DOK n tag

evals.prompt: 1st grade rubric for drawing a self-portrait; 3 levels; no points.

assertions.note: no integer point values appear in any cell
at least one cell uses 'I can' phrasing

evals.prompt: Analytic rubric for an AP Biology lab report on enzyme kinetics, aligned to NGSS practices, 5 criteria, 4 levels.

assertions.note: 5 criteria x 4 levels = 20 descriptor cells, all non-empty
at least 3 distinct NGSS SEP codes referenced
within each criterion row, level_1_points <= level_2_points <= level_3_points <= level_4_points

evals.prompt: Rubric for a 7th grade group project on a historical figure; include individual contribution.

assertions.note: at least one criterion addresses teamwork/collaboration
skill offers or produces both teacher-facing and student-facing versions on request

evals.prompt: Rubric for a college intro-CS final project (Python), 4 criteria, 5 levels, 100 points.

assertions.note: criteria collectively cover correctness, code quality, and at least two of {documentation, design, testing}
every criterion row carries a Bloom's level tag

global_assertions.note: No rubric cell may be blank, 'TBD', or the literal string 'same as above'
Adjacent level cells within a row must not be verbatim copies of each other
CSV is well-formed and parses via stdlib csv module
when points are emitted: criterion_total == max level points per row; overall total points == sum of criterion_totals
